config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama supaya kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikorbankan, masyarakat justru berisiko menjadi tidak adil dan tidak aman.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, history makes me wary of letting "the common good" override personal liberty, because that language has too often been used to justify discrimination, censorship, and government overreach. At the same time, I do think there are narrow cases like public health or emergency safety where limited restrictions can be justified, but only if they are clearly necessary, temporary, and accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, apalagi kalau pengawasan lemah dan suara minoritas mudah diabaikan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih sehat kalau dibatasi oleh hak dasar, karena tanpa itu masyarakat bisa berubah menjadi alat untuk menekan orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Pembatasan hak hanya layak jika benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan secara ketat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should generally take priority over individual rights, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse abuses that were later defended as "necessary." Collective goals matter, but they should be pursued within firm limits that protect speech, due process, religion, and equal treatment, especially for minorities who are most vulnerable when the majority gets to define the public good. I am comfortable with narrow, temporary limits in genuine emergencies, but only when they are tightly justified and subject to real oversight. If society can override rights whenever it claims a good reason, then rights are not rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, minoritas, atau siapa pun yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibangun di atas hak dasar, bukan menginjaknya, karena kalau tidak, yang terjadi sering bukan ketertiban melainkan penyalahgunaan wewenang. Pembatasan hak hanya bisa dibenarkan kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, sementara, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau hak individu bisa dikesampingkan setiap kali mayoritas mengaku punya alasan baik, maka hak itu kehilangan maknanya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general principle, because from an American perspective it is too easy for "society" to become a vague excuse for trampling speech, due process, religion, and minority rights. The strongest version of a free society is not one where individuals are sacrificed for the majority, but one where collective goals are pursued within hard legal limits that protect people from government abuse. I do think narrow emergency restrictions can be justified, but only when they are temporary, necessary, and tightly overseen. If rights can be overridden whenever the majority says it is for the common good, then those rights are not real protections.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
